name: API Productization

useCases.use: Packaging order management APIs for retailer partner self-service integration
useCases.dontUse: Internal-only utilities or helper APIs not intended for broad consumption

useCases.use: Creating tiered API products (Bronze/Silver/Gold) with different rate limits and SLAs
useCases.dontUse: Experimental or prototype APIs that are not yet stable for external consumption

useCases.use: Developer portal onboarding for McCain's digital supply chain partner ecosystem
useCases.dontUse: Machine-to-machine integrations already governed by specific partner contracts

approvers: Digital Platform Team, Integration Architecture Board
components: Azure API Management Developer Portal — self-service API discovery and onboarding
Azure API Management Products — API grouping, access tiers, and subscription management
OpenAPI Specification (3.0+) — standardized API contract documentation
Azure AD B2C — partner identity management and API subscription authentication
Azure Monitor & API Analytics — usage tracking, SLA monitoring, and billing metrics
Azure DevOps Pipelines — automated API documentation generation and portal publishing

guardrails.rule: All externally-consumed APIs must be published as managed API Products
API Products must have OpenAPI 3.0+ specification published to Developer Portal
Each API Product tier must have defined and monitored SLA targets
API subscription keys must have maximum 180-day expiry
API Products must provide sandbox environment for partner testing

revisionHistory.author: Emma Larsson
revisionHistory.summary: Initial draft with tiered product model, Developer Portal integration, and SLA framework

strategicIntent: Package internal APIs as managed products with defined SLAs, comprehensive documentation, tiered access plans, and self-service developer onboarding to accelerate partner integration and internal API reuse across McCain's digital ecosystem.
exceptionProcess: Submit Digital Platform Team request for APIs consumed externally without API Product packaging. Must include documentation plan, SLA commitment, and partner onboarding process.
interactionSteps: API team publishes OpenAPI spec and creates API Product in API Management
Product is configured with access tiers (Bronze: 100 req/min, Silver: 1000, Gold: 10000)
SLA targets are defined per tier and configured in Azure Monitor
API documentation is auto-generated from OpenAPI spec and published to Developer Portal
Partner registers on Developer Portal and subscribes to appropriate product tier
Subscription keys are issued and partner completes sandbox testing
API usage metrics track consumption against SLA and trigger alerts on threshold breach
problemStatement: Internal APIs are built as point solutions without documentation, SLAs, or onboarding processes. Partners and internal teams spend weeks discovering, understanding, and gaining access to APIs, slowing time-to-market for new integrations and digital products.
securityConsiderations: Developer Portal must require Azure AD B2C authentication for all access
API subscription keys must have configurable expiry and rotation policies
Sandbox and production environments must be completely isolated
API documentation must not expose internal implementation details or infrastructure information
Partner access must be revocable immediately in case of security incident
